title: Transactions (BEGIN, COMMIT, ROLLBACK)

overview.what: A transaction groups multiple SQL statements into a single, all-or-nothing logical unit of work. You start with BEGIN. If all commands succeed, you COMMIT to save them permanently. If something fails, you ROLLBACK to undo all changes made since BEGIN, leaving the database exactly as it was.
overview.why: Without transactions, a failure halfway through a complex operation (like deducting money from one account but failing to add it to another) leaves the database in an inconsistent, corrupt state.

why_learning_this: You can guarantee data integrity even when hardware or network failures occur.
You can safely test potentially destructive queries by wrapping them in a transaction and rolling back.

common_mistakes.title: Forgetting to COMMIT
common_mistakes.explanation: If you issue a BEGIN but never COMMIT, your changes remain invisible to other users and will eventually be discarded when your session ends.

common_mistakes.title: Catching errors but not rolling back
common_mistakes.explanation: In Postgres, if a query inside a transaction causes an error, the entire transaction becomes aborted. You MUST issue a ROLLBACK before you can run new queries in that session.

recall_questions.q: What command makes the changes in a transaction permanent?
What happens if a transaction is rolled back?
recall_questions.answer: All changes made within that transaction are discarded, returning the data to its state before the transaction began.

understanding_checks.question: What rows does the final SELECT query return?
understanding_checks.why: The update was wrapped in a transaction that was rolled back, so the price reverted to its original value of 49.99.

understanding_checks.question: What rows does the final SELECT query return?
understanding_checks.why: The transaction was committed successfully, making both deletions permanent.

query_walkthrough.focus: Both updates act as a single unit. Either both employees have their salaries adjusted, or neither do.

practice_tasks.title: Rollback a mistake
practice_tasks.prompt: You accidentally deleted all customers! Add the command needed to undo the deletion before it becomes permanent.

challenge.title: Complete the transfer
challenge.prompt: Write a transaction that starts a block, updates the status of order id 3 to 'cancelled', then commits the changes.
